prompt:
PR #14 in gunk-dev/the-valley ('Re-derive design/roadmap.md against the scenario ladder') is approved in substance; your job is a small residue fixup on its branch (agent/20260706-1328-28c3e092) before it merges. This is a design-docs repo; all edits are markdown. The owner explicitly wants MINIMAL diffs — no detail bloat, no rewording beyond what is listed.

FIRST: update the branch from origin/main (merge is fine; files are disjoint so it is clean). Two PRs just landed on main that this fixup depends on: #9 added the decision node .the-valley/decisions/dcr-db1acbb-hetzner-replication-mechanism.md and flipped oc-fc348f0 to done; #13 reshaped design/requirements.md so need 7 is now 'Demand-shaped work'. Verify both are present on main before editing.

THE EDITS (all in this PR's branch):

1. design/roadmap.md, Phase 0 durability paragraph ('The mechanism is decided (it was this document's open question)'): add a link to the decision record so the claim is auditable. Convention: design docs cite knowledge nodes with RELATIVE markdown links to the node file (see design/architecture.md linking ../.the-valley/ideas/ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md), NEVER [[wiki]] syntax. Use [dcr-db1acbb](../.the-valley/decisions/dcr-db1acbb-hetzner-replication-mechanism.md).

2. design/roadmap.md, 'Open questions' section, the 'Offsite replication mechanism — decided.' bullet: link dcr-db1acbb here too. Also REMOVE the sentence saying the corresponding openquestions.md entry 'predates the decision and belongs under *Resolved*' — because edit 4 below actually moves it, making that sentence false.

3. design/roadmap.md: the ZFS rejection currently reads 'was considered and rejected' — restore the decision node's provisionality: 'was considered and rejected for now'. If the open-questions bullet also says 'rejected', align it the same way.

4. design/openquestions.md: move the 'Hetzner backup mechanism' entry out of the open list into the existing 'Resolved (kept for the record)' section, with a one-line resolution matching that section's style: decided 2026-07-04 as push-triggered git-native mirror plus nightly restic offsite backup, ZFS send rejected for now — link [dcr-db1acbb](../.the-valley/decisions/dcr-db1acbb-hetzner-replication-mechanism.md).

5. design/user-scenarios.md, S1 'Durability means pushed = replicated' scope bullet: the sentence 'The mechanism is an open question; candidates and tradeoffs live in the [roadmap Phase 0 table](./roadmap.md#phase-0--mvp-repos-off-github), not here.' is now false (mechanism decided; this PR deletes the candidates table). Rewrite minimally and keep this doc problem-space (zero mechanism detail), e.g.: 'The mechanism is decided ([dcr-db1acbb](../.the-valley/decisions/dcr-db1acbb-hetzner-replication-mechanism.md)); the chosen layers live in [roadmap Phase 0](./roadmap.md#phase-0--mvp-repos-off-github), not here.'

6. design/roadmap.md, directly after the existing 'S7 (strangers) has no phase, deliberately' note under the at-a-glance table: add ONE sentence acknowledging that requirements need 7 ('Demand-shaped work', design/requirements.md) is also deliberately phase-less — phases serve rungs (rule 4) and need 7 derives from the README's outcome-engine framing rather than a rung, though Phase 4 (dispatch targets an outcome node) and Phase 5 (a landed change closes the outcome it serves) partially serve it. One sentence, mirroring the S7 note's tone.

7. design/roadmap.md, Phase 0 exit criteria: it claims to restate S1's acceptance checklist 'verbatim' but actually paraphrases (it drops 'including one agent dispatched against a node instead of a GitHub issue' from the in-repo-node item). Either drop the word 'verbatim' or add the missing sub-clause — whichever is the smaller diff.

ACCEPTANCE: every relative link and anchor in the three changed files resolves against the branch's tree post-merge-from-main; run 'nix flake check' and ensure it passes; diff limited to design/roadmap.md, design/openquestions.md, design/user-scenarios.md plus the merge-from-main commit. Push to the PR branch (updates PR #14) and leave a PR comment listing the residue fixes applied.
